message:
fix(rch): use configured socket path in send_daemon_command

Previously send_daemon_command() hardcoded DEFAULT_SOCKET_PATH, ignoring
the user's configured socket location in ~/.config/rch/config.toml.

Now loads config and uses config.general.socket_path with tilde expansion,
consistent with how the rest of the codebase handles socket paths.

This fixes issues where users with custom socket locations couldn't use
CLI commands that communicate with the daemon (status, workers, etc.).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
